2605.13536.title: HLS-Seek: QoR-Aware Code Generation for High-Level Synthesis via Proxy Comparative Reward Reinforcement Learning
2605.13536.paper_summary: High-Level Synthesis (HLS) compiles algorithmic C/C++ descriptions into hardware, with Quality of Results (QoR) -- latency and resource utilization -- critically governed by pragma configurations and code structure. Existing LLM-based HLS approaches train for functional correctness but ignore QoR entirely. We observe that reinforcement learning (RL) for HLS does not require absolute synthesis results -- only relative comparisons between candidates. Based on this insight, we propose \textbf{HLS-Seek}, a QoR-aware NL-to-HLS framework that replaces expensive synthesis-in-the-loop RL with a comparative proxy reward model achieving 99.53\% Pareto-dominance accuracy. To prevent reward hacking, we introduce \textit{uncertainty-aware Monte Carlo (MC) dropout switching} that selectively invokes real Vitis HLS synthesis for low-confidence candidates and online updates the proxy, creating a self-improving reward system. HLS-Seek achieves 81.5\% syntax correctness pass@1 and 81.4\% Func@5 on HLS-eval with only 7B parameters, surpassing GPT-5.1 and other frontier models while achieving 8.5$\times$ faster training than real-reward RL. On QoR evaluation, HLS-Seek achieves the lowest latency on 16/30 kernels and Pareto-dominates HLS-specific baselines on 9 kernels.
2605.13536.paper_summary_zh: 高階合成（HLS）將算法 C/C++ 描述編譯為硬體，其結果質量（QoR）——延遲和資源利用率——受到 pragma 配置和代碼結構的嚴格控制。現有的基於 LLM 的 HLS 方法訓練以實現功能正確性，但完全忽略 QoR。我們觀察到，HLS 的強化學習（RL）不需要絕對的合成結果——只需要候選者之間的相對比較。基於這一見解，我們提出了 \textbf{HLS-Seek}，這是一個關注 QoR 的 NL-to-HLS 框架，將昂貴的合成循環 RL 替換為一種比較代理獎勵模型，實現 99.53\% 的 Pareto 優勢準確率。為了防止獎勵操縱，我們引入了 \textit{不確定性感知的蒙特卡羅（MC）隨機失活切換}，該方法選擇性地對低信心候選者調用真實的 Vitis HLS 合成，並在線更新代理，創建一個自我改善的獎勵系統。HLS-Seek 在 HLS-eval 上達到 81.5\% 的語法正確性 pass@1 和 81.4\% 的 Func@5，僅使用 7B 參數，超越了 GPT-5.1 和其他前沿模型，同時訓練速度比真實獎勵 RL 快 8.5$\times$。在 QoR 評估中，HLS-Seek 在 30 個內核中的 16 個上實現了最低延遲，並在 9 個內核上優於 HLS 特定基準。
2605.13536.author: Qingyun Zou et.al.
2605.13536.authors: Qingyun Zou, Feng Yu, Hongshi Tan, Yao Chen, Bingsheng He, WengFai Wong
